original_image_tags: A typewritten telex message on a "FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE COMMUNICATIONS SECTION" form, dated DEC 29 1948, with the header "TELEMETER". The page contains handwritten annotations, stamps, and a distribution list in the upper right. The main text details an investigation into a "flying disk" report from Lonnie Edward Noack, concluding that parts found were from a model tow target constructed by Claude L. Wolford. There are several redaction marks over words like "COMPASS" and "PLASTICS MOLDING CO., LA.".

elements.description: A scanned page of a typed government communication. The page contains typed body text, multiple red rubber stamps (including 'FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION', 'DEC 29 1948', 'TELEMETER', and 'RECORDED'), handwritten annotations in pen and pencil, a red routing list box with names on the right side, and two punch holes at the top. There are no photographs, sketches, diagrams, maps, or charts present.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree the page contains no photographs, sketches, diagrams, maps, or charts, and both describe the same non-photographic visual elements (stamps, annotations, routing list) with no contradictions.
